title: Elastomeric propylene-alpha-olefin-diene terpolymer compositions
abstract: A propylene-Î±-olefin-diene (PEDM) terpolymer may comprise 75 to 95 wt% propylene, 5 to 20 wt% Î±-olefin, and 0.5 to 5 wt% diene, said wt% based on the weight of the PEDM terpolymer. The propylene-Î±-olefin-diene terpolymer may be blended with an ethylene- based copolymer and optionally a variety of additives to form an elastomeric composition. An exemplary elastomeric composition includes 5 to 40 parts by weight per hundred parts by weight rubber (phr) of the PEDM terpolymer, 60 to 95 phr of the ethylene-based copolymer, and optionally additives like carbon black, zinc dimethacrylate, paraffinic oil, zinc oxide, and/or zinc stearate.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An elastomeric composition comprising: from 5 to 40 parts by weight per hundred parts by weight rubber (phr) of a propylene-a-olefin-diene (PEDM) terpolymer comprising 75 to 95 wt% propylene, 5 to 20 wt% a-olefin, and 0.5 to 5 wt% diene, said wt% based on the weight of the PEDM terpolymer; and from 60 to 95 phr of an ethylene -based copolymer comprising ethylene, one or more C 3 to Ci 2 a-olefins, and, optionally, one or more dienes; wherein an ethylene content of the ethylene -based copolymer (in wt% on the basis of total weight of the ethylene-based copolymer) is different from an ethylene content of the PEDM terpolymer (in wt% on the basis of total weight of the PEDM terpolymer). 2. The composition of claim 1, wherein the PEDM comprises two or more propylene- based copolymers. 3. The composition of claim 1, wherein the ethylene -based copolymer comprises two or more ethylene-based copolymers. 4. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the PEDM comprises two or more propylene-based copolymers and the ethylene -based copolymer comprises two or more ethylene-based copolymers. 5. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the PEDM is formed by polymerizing the propylene, the ethylene, and the diene in the presence of a catalyst compound represented by formula (I): TyCpâmMGnXq (I) , wherein: Cp' is a tetrahydroindacenyl group (such as tetrahydro-.v-indacenyl or tetrahydro-c/.v- indacenyl) which is optionally substituted or unsubstituted, provided that when Cp' is tetrahydro-.v-i ndacenyl : 1) the 3 and/or 4 positions are not aryl or substituted aryl, 2) the 3 position is not directly bonded to a group 15 or 16 heteroatom, 3) there are no additional rings fused to the tetrahydroindacenyl ligand, 4) T is not bonded to the 2-position, and 5) the 5, 6, or 

 7-position (preferably the 6 position) is geminally disubstituted, preferably with two Ci-Cio alkyl groups; M is a group 3, 4, 5, or 6 transition metal, preferably group 4 transition metal, for example titanium, zirconium, or hafnium; T is a bridging group; y is 0 or 1, indicating the absence or presence of T; G is a heteroatom group represented by the formula JR' z-y where J is N, P, O or S, R 1 is a Ci to Cioo hydrocarbyl group, and z is 2 when J is N or P, and z is 1 when J is O or S; X is a leaving group; and m = l; n = l, 2 or 3; q = l, 2 or 3; and the sum of m+n+q is equal to the oxidation state of the transition metal (preferably 2, 3, 4, 5, or 6, preferably 4). 6. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the composition has a rubber-to- rubber tack force exceeding 150 g of force/inch width. 7. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the diene of the PEDM terpolymer is one or more of 5-ethylidene-2-norbornene, dicyclopentadiene, 5-vinyl-2-norbornene, 1,4- hexadiene, alkyldicyclopentadiene, l,4-pentadiene, l,5-hexadiene, l,4-heptadiene, 2-methyl- l,5-hexadiene, cyclooctadiene, l,4-octadiene, l,7-octadiene, 5-n-propylidene-2-norbomene, 5-(2-methyl-2-butenyl)-2-norborene, and norbomadiene. 8. The composition of claim 7, wherein the diene of the PEDM terpolymer is 5-ethylidene- 2-norbomene. 9. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the ethylene -based copolymer comprises 40 to 95 wt% ethylene, 0 to 10 wt% of one or more dienes, and a Ci to Ci 2 a-olefin, said wt%s based on the total weight of the ethylene-based copolymer. 10. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the C 3 to Ci 2 a-olefin of the ethylene- based copolymer is propylene or 1 -butene. 11. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the C 3 to Ci 2 a-olefin of the ethylene- based copolymer is propylene. 12. The composition of any preceding claim, further comprising carbon black at 1 phr to 500 phr. 13. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the PEDM terpolymer has an H f less than 1 J/g. 14. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the PEDM terpolymer has a crystallinity of from <5%. 15. An at least partially crosslinked elastomeric composition that is the reaction product of: the composition according to any preceding claim; one or more vulcanization activators; and one or more vulcanizing agents. 16. The composition of claim 15, comprising from 1 phr to 150 phr of a paraffinic process oil, the paraffinic process oil having a viscosity at 40Â°C within the range from 80 to 600 CSt. 17. The composition of any preceding claim having one or more of the following properties: (a) a tack of about 4,000 Pa or greater; (b) a green strength 25% modulus of about 1.7 MPa or greater; and (c) a tensile strength at break of 21.4 MPa or greater. 18. A belt comprising the composition according to any preceding claim. 19. An article comprising the composition of any preceding claim, wherein the composition adheres to at least one polyester substrate selected from the group consisting of: a polyester film, a polyester cord, a polyester woven fabric, and a polyester nonwoven fabric. 20. The composition of any preceding claim further comprising an additive selected from the group consisting of: a filler, a process oil, a plasticizer, a compatibilizer, a crosslinker, and any combination thereof. 21. A method of producing the composition of any preceding claim comprising the steps of: producing a master batch at a first temperature, the master batch comprising the ethylene-based copolymer, the PEDM terpolymer, and the additive; mixing a curative and/or a crosslinking agent into the master batch at a second temperature that is lower than the first temperature; and recovering the composition. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein producing the compound master batch-comprises mixing the ethylene-based copolymer and the additive until the additive is incorporated before adding the PEDM in a single pass. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein producing the compound master batch comprises: mixing in a first pass the ethylene-based copolymer and the additive until the additive is incorporated; and mixing in a second pass the PEDM terpolymer with a curative and/or a crosslinking agent into a product of the first pass. 24. The method of claims 21-23, wherein the additive comprises at least one selected from the group consisting of: paraffinic oil, zinc oxide, and zinc stearate. 25. An elastomeric composition comprising: from 5 to 40 parts by weight per hundred parts by weight rubber (phr) of a propylene-a-olefin-diene (PEDM) terpolymer comprising 75 to 95 wt% propylene, 4 to 20 wt% a-olefin, and 0.5 to 10 wt% diene, said wt% based on the weight of the PEDM terpolymer; and from 60 to 95 phr of an ethylene -based copolymer comprising ethylene, one or more C 3 to Ci 2 a-olefins, and, optionally, one or more dienes; wherein an ethylene content of the ethylene -based copolymer (in wt% on the basis of total weight of the ethylene-based 

 copolymer) is different from an ethylene content of the PEDM terpolymer (in wt% on the basis of total weight of the PEDM terpolymer). 26. The composition of claim 25, wherein the PEDM comprises two or more propylene- based copolymers. 27. The composition of claim 25, wherein the ethylene-based copolymer comprises two or more ethylene-based copolymers. 28. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the PEDM comprises two or more propylene-based copolymers and the ethylene -based copolymer comprises two or more ethylene-based copolymers. 29. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the PEDM is formed by polymerizing the propylene, the ethylene, and the diene in the presence of a catalyst compound represented by formula (I): TyCpâmMGnXq (I) , wherein: Cp' is a tetrahydroindacenyl group (such as tetrahydro-.v-indacenyl or tetrahydro-i/.v- indacenyl) which is optionally substituted or unsubstituted, provided that when Cp' is tetrahydro-.v-i ndacenyl : 1) the 3 and/or 4 positions are not aryl or substituted aryl, 2) the 3 position is not directly bonded to a group 15 or 16 heteroatom, 3) there are no additional rings fused to the tetrahydroindacenyl ligand, 4) T is not bonded to the 2-position, and 5) the 5, 6, or 7-position (preferably the 6 position) is geminally disubstituted, preferably with two Ci-Cio alkyl groups; M is a group 3, 4, 5, or 6 transition metal, preferably group 4 transition metal, for example titanium, zirconium, or hafnium; T is a bridging group; y is 0 or 1, indicating the absence or presence of T; G is a heteroatom group represented by the formula JR y where J is N, P, O or S, R 1 is a Ci to Cioo hydrocarbyl group, and z is 2 when J is N or P, and z is 1 when J is O or S; 

 X is a leaving group; and m = l; n = l, 2 or 3; q = l, 2 or 3; and the sum of m+n+q is equal to the oxidation state of the transition metal (preferably 2, 3, 4, 5, or 6, preferably 4). 30. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the diene of the PEDM terpolymer is one or more of 5-ethylidene-2-norbornene, dicyclopentadiene, 5-vinyl-2-norbornene, 1,4- hexadiene, alkyldicyclopentadiene, l,4-pentadiene, l,5-hexadiene, l,4-heptadiene, 2-methyl- l,5-hexadiene, cyclooctadiene, l,4-octadiene, l,7-octadiene, 5-n-propylidene-2-norbomene, 5-(2-methyl-2-butenyl)-2-norborene, and norbomadiene. 31. The composition of claim 30, wherein the diene of the PEDM terpolymer is 5- ethylidene-2-norbomene. 32. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the ethylene -based copolymer comprises 40 to 95 wt% ethylene, 0 to 10 wt% of one or more dienes, and a C 3 to Ci 2 a-olefin, said wt%s based on the total weight of the ethylene-based copolymer. 33. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the C 3 to Ci 2 a-olefin of the ethylene- based copolymer is propylene or 1 -butene. 34. The composition of any preceding claim, wherein the C 3 to Ci 2 a-olefin of the ethylene- based copolymer is propylene.
